mechanism: The basis trade suppresses term premium by approximately 20-40bp through leveraged Treasury demand. A basis trade unwind would release this suppression, adding to QT's direct term premium decompression. Conversely, QT-driven repo rate volatility is the most likely trigger for basis trade unwind.
regime_dependence: Active while basis trade is large (~$800B-$1T). Unwind would compound QT term premium effects. Continued basis trade growth partially offsets QT decompression. The 20-40bp suppression estimate lacks an identified natural experiment.
